licence.p: This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 Unported Licence. To view a copy of the licence, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ or send a letter to Creative Commons, 444 Castro Street, Suite 900, Mountain View, California, 94041, USA.

projectDesc.p: The project DHARMA has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union's Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no 809994).

prefixDef.p: Public URIs with the prefix bib to point to a Zotero Group Library named ERC-DHARMA whose data are open to the public.
Internal URIs using the part prefix to point to person elements in the DHARMA_idListMembers.xml file.

div.p: * OmŐź namo nńĀrńĀyaŠĻáńĀya.

lg.l: vidyud yatra maŠĻái-dyutiŠł• phaŠĻái-pater vńĀlendur indrńĀyudhaŠĻĀ
bhŇęyńĀd vaŠł• sa bhavńĀrtti-tńĀpa-bhiduraŠł• ŇõambhoŠł• kaparddńĀmvu-daŠł•.

kahlńĀre hata-mohyatńĀ
rati-patńĀv eko ‚Äôham eveti dhńęŠł•,

yasyńĀmńę AmrŐ•tńĀtmanaŠł• samudayanty ńĀŇõu prakńĀ
ŇõńĀj jagaty

yaŇõobhiŠł• Ňõobhante paridhi-pari
ŠĻáaddhńĀ Iva diŇõaŠł•,

yaŇõ cetoma
yam eva Ňõaurya-vijayńę datvauŠĻ£adhaŠĻĀ taT-kŠĻ£aŠĻáńĀd

akŠĻ£ńęŠĻáńĀ racayńĀ√Ī cakńĀra vaŇõagńĀŠł•
svasmin¬∑ pareŠĻ£ńĀŠĻĀ ŇõriyaŠł•.

-svantńĀ yńĀ
ntu kathan na nńĀma ripavas tasya prayogńĀl layam¬∑.
advaitena yatas tato ‚Äôpi sa paro devaŠł• paraŠĻĀ vńękŠĻ£yate..

div.p:
sa khalu Ňõrńę-vikra
mapura-samńĀvńĀsita-Ňõrńęmaj-jaya-skandhńĀvńĀrńĀT mahńĀrńĀjńĀdhirńĀja-Ňõrńę-vallńĀlasena
-deva-pńĀdńĀnudhyńĀta-parameŇõvara-paramavaiŠĻ£ŠĻáava-paramabhaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠ńĀraka-mahńĀrńĀjńĀdhirńĀja
-Ňõrńęmal-lakŠĻ£maŠĻáasena-devaŠł• kuŇõalńę. samupagatńĀŇõeŠĻ£a-rńĀja-rńĀjanyaka-rńĀj√Īńę
-rńĀŠĻáaka-rńĀjaputra-rńĀjńĀmńĀtya-purohita-mahńĀdharmmńĀdhyakŠĻ£a-mahńĀsńĀndhivigrahi
ka-mahńĀsenńĀpati-mahńĀmudrńĀdhikrŐ•ta-ńÄntaraŠĻÖga-vrŐ•haduparika-mahńĀkŠĻ£apaŠĻ≠alika
-mahńĀpratńęhńĀra-mahńĀbhogika-mahńĀpńęlupati-mahńĀgaŠĻáastha-daussńĀdhika-cauro
ddharaŠĻáika-nauvalahastyaŇõvagomahiŠĻ£ńĀjńĀvikńĀdivyńĀprŐ•taka-gaulmika-daŠĻáŠłćapńĀŇõi
ka-daŠĻáŠłćanńĀyaka-viŠĻ£ayapatvy-ńĀdńęn ananyńĀŠĻĀŇõ ca sakala-rńĀja-pńĀdopajńęvino ‚ÄôdhyakŠĻ£a-pra
cńĀroktńĀn ihńĀkńęrttitńĀn¬∑ caŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠a-bhaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠a-jńĀtńęyńĀn¬∑ janapradńĀn¬∑ kŠĻ£etrakarńĀŠĻĀŇõ ca vrńĀ
hmaŠĻáńĀn¬∑ vrńĀhmaŠĻáottarńĀn¬∑ yathńĀrhaŠĻĀ mńĀnayati vodhayati samńĀdiŇõati ca.

matam a
stu bhavatńĀŠĻĀ. yathńĀ Ňõrńę-pauŠĻáŠłćravarddhana-bhukty-antaŠł•pńĀti-varendryńĀn¬∑* pŇęrvve vuddha-vihńĀrńę-deva
tńĀ-nikara-deyńĀrmmaŠĻáa-bhŇęmy-ńĀŠłćhńĀvńĀpa-pŇęrvvńĀliŠł• sńęmńĀ. dakŠĻ£iŠĻáe nicaŠłća-hńĀra-pu
ŠĻ£kariŠĻáńę sńęmńĀ. paŇõcime nandiharipńĀ-kuŠĻáŠłćńę sńęmńĀ. Uttare mollńĀŠĻáa-khńĀŠłćńę sńęmńĀ. I
tthaŠĻĀ catuŠł•-sńęmńĀvacchinnas tatratya-deŇõa-vyavahńĀra-nalena deva-go-pathńĀdy-asńĀra-bhŇę-vahiŠł•
pa√ĪconmńĀnńĀdhika-viŠĻĀŇõaty-uttarńĀŠłćhńĀvńĀpa-ŇõataikńĀtmakaŠł• saŠĻĀvvatsareŠĻáa kaparddaka-pu
rńĀŠĻáa-sńĀrddha-Ňõataikotpattiko velahiŠĻ£ŠĻ≠ńęgrńĀmńęya-bhŇę-bhńĀgaŠł• sa-jhńĀŠĻ≠a-viŠĻ≠apaŠł•
sa-jala-sthalaŠł• sa-garttoŠĻ£araŠł• sa-guvńĀka-nńĀrikelaŠł• sahya-daŇõńĀparńĀdhaŠł• pari
rhrŐ•ta-sarvva-pńęŠłćo ‚ÄôcaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠a-bhaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠a-praveŇõo ‚Äôki√ĪciT-pragrńĀhyas trŐ•ŠĻáa-pŇęti-gocara
-paryantaŠł• hutńĀŇõanadevaŇõarmmaŠĻáaŠł• prapautrńĀya mńĀrkkaŠĻáŠłćeyadevaŇõarmmaŠĻáaŠł• pautrńĀya la
kŠĻ£mńędharadevaŇõarmmaŠĻáaŠł• putrńĀya bharadvńĀja-sagotrńĀya bhńĀradvńĀja-ńÄŠĻÖgirasa
-vńĀrhaspatya-pravarńĀya sńĀmaveda-kauthuma-ŇõńĀkhńĀ-caraŠĻáńĀnuŠĻ£ŠĻ≠hńĀyine hemńĀŇõva-ra
tha-mahńĀ-dńĀnńĀcńĀrya-Ňõrńę-ń™ŇõvaradevaŇõarmmaŠĻáe puŠĻáye ‚Äôhani vidhivad udaka-pŇę
rvvakaŠĻĀ bhagavantaŠĻĀ Ňõrńęman-nńĀrńĀyaŠĻáa-bhaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠ńĀrakam uddiŇõya mńĀtńĀ-pitror ńĀtmanaŇõ ca pu
ŠĻáya-yaŇõo ‚ÄôbhivrŐ•ddhaye datta-hemńĀŇõyva-dratha-mahńĀ-dńĀne dakŠĻ£iŠĻáńĀtvenotsrŐ•jya ńÄ
-candrńĀrkka-kŠĻ£iti-sama-kńĀlaŠĻĀ yńĀvaT bhŇęmi-cchidra-nyńĀyena tńĀmra-ŇõńĀsanńę-krŐ•tya pra
datto ‚ÄôsmńĀbhiŠł•. tad bhavadbhiŠł• sarvvair evńĀnumatntavyam¬∑. bhńĀvibhir api nrŐ•pati
bhir apaharaŠĻáe naraka-pńĀta-bhyńĀT pńĀlane dharmma-gauravńĀt pńĀlanńęyam¬∑.

bha
vanti cńĀtra dharmmńĀnuŇõaŠĻĀsinaŠł• ŇõlokńĀŠł•,

lg.l: yasya yasya yadńĀ bhŇęmis tasya tasya tadńĀ phalaŠĻĀ.

bhŇęmiŠĻĀ yaŠł• pratigrŐ•hnńĀti yaŇõ ca bhŇęmiŠĻĀ praya
cchati,

sva-dattńĀŠĻĀ para-dattńĀm vńĀ yo hare
ta vasundharńĀm¬∑,
sa viŠĻ£ŠĻ≠hńĀyńĀŠĻĀ krŐ•mir bhŇętvńĀ pitrŐ•bhis saha pacyate.

div.p: saŠĻĀ 2 bhńĀdra-dine 28
Ňõrńę ni mahńĀsńĀŠĻĀ ni .

app.note: Banerji takes Šł• as a sign of interpunction.
Banerji takes Šł• as a sign of interpunction.
As suggested by Banerji and Majumdar, this seems to be a kńĀkapada symbol indicating the attempt of emendation by the scribe, who seems to have forgotten to insert the correction.
Majumdar suggests that mmaŠĻáa is a substitute for mńĀla
Need to be dŇętakaŠĻĀ for metrical consideration.

div.p: Success! OŠĻĀ, homage to NńĀrńĀyaŠĻáa!

In which flash is like brilliance of the jewel of the Lord of Snakes ŇöeŠĻ£a, the new moon is like a rainbow, water is the River of Heaven GaŠĻÖgńĀ, the garland of white skulls is like a row of swans, breath is suppressed by a habit of meditation, and elevation of hairs is superior. May that cloud-like knotted hair of Ňöambhu become the one destroying grief of existence by heat for you!

The delight in the ocean, the closeness to cutting of sorrow among a flock of partridges, the destroyed delusion in a waterlily, the reflection as ‚ÄúI am the only one‚ÄĚ in the Husband of Pleasure KńĀma. These rise immediately in this world from lustre of that one made of nectar the Moon. May that light of the Moon, bowing to the tradition of meditation by Atri, stay in the happy person!

With creepers that were lustre of their foot nails shining by water that was beams from crowns of a crore of kings bowed for service, the enjoyers of the earth kings, removing burns caused by poison that was energy of their enemies, were then clearly born in the lineage of the Lord of Plants the Moon.

By snows that were the fame of his arms, spreading as far as the KaumńĀra Mountain and flowing in various regions, he exhibited withering of lotuses that were faces of enemy kings. Hemantasena, who had ripe and dense praiseworthy characters furnished with a series of merits from land and stream that was the Sena lineage, belonging to the lineage of them kings of the lunar lineage, was just distinctly born.

Even now, quarters shine by fame belonging to him, going with accumulated energy of his arms, as if they are wrapped with a garment. Then that Vijayasena, the victor, the husband of the earth surrounded by waves of the four oceans skillful in play of girdles, was born.

Then VallńĀlasena, the obstacle for the completion of the Kali Age, an active sole traveller on the path of the Vedas, and like the army with shapes in both station and moving, was born. He, the brave winner, after giving medicine made of his heart at the same moment, by his eye made the Fortune of enemy kings into an obedient wife on himself.

Due to desire for enjoyment of the characters of a group of women in other quarters to be traversed, by the lords of quarters filled with such and such powers, he was produced by bestowal of their parts. Hence King illustrious LakŠĻ£amaŠĻáasena, who had liquid of poison that was his enemy suppressed by heat of his arms, who was the abode of the duties of royal class, and whose boundary was benevolence, was born.

Why then should not enemies deceptions, deprived of their territories liberated from objects of senses and having a good end by being killed by made devoted to him only, because of fear of endless imprisonment eternal bondage, go to destruction rest due to contrivance of connection with him? By them, wherever, on their own mirrored image reflection on ńĀtman, also on a descending letter falling leaf, and on moving grass, nothing but that highest king god was looked at, without doubt with non-duality.

From the illustrious camp of victory pitched at illustrious Vikramapura. ParameŇõvara, devout worshiper of ViŠĻ£ŠĻáu, paramabhaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠ńĀraka mahńĀrńĀjńĀdhirńĀja illustrious LakŠĻ£maŠĻáasenadeva, healthy, accepted by his majesty mahńĀrńĀjńĀdhirńĀja illustrious VallńĀlasena, honours, announces and orders suitably all the approached rńĀja, rńĀjanyaka, rńĀj√Īńę, rńĀŠĻáaka, rńĀjaputra, rńĀjńĀmńĀtya, purohita, mahńĀdharmńĀdhyakŠĻ£a, mahńĀsńĀndhivigrahika, mahńĀsenńĀpati, mahńĀmudrńĀdhikrŐ•ta, ńĀntaraŠĻÖga, brŐ•haduparika, mahńĀkŠĻ£apaŠĻ≠alika, mahńĀpratńęhńĀra, mahńĀbhogika, mahńĀpńęlupati, mahńĀgaŠĻáastha, daussńĀdhika, cauroddharaŠĻáika, official in charge of fleet, elephants, horses, cows, buffaloes, goat, sheep and so on, gaulmika, daŠĻáŠłćapńĀŇõika, daŠĻáŠłćanńĀyaka, viŠĻ£ayapati and so on, and all other dependants on the royal feet, those declared of positions of officials, unnamed here, the ones belonging to caŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠as and bhaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠as, people and cultivators, brńĀhmaŠĻáas and best brńĀhmaŠĻáas as follows:

‚ÄúIt should be approved by you. In Varendrńę belonging to illustrious PauŠĻáŠłćravardhana bhukti. To the east, the eastern embankment of 1 ńĀŠłćhńĀvńĀpa of land for 1 armaŠĻáa droŠĻáa of production to be given to a group of deities of the Buddha vihńĀrńę is the border; to the south, the pond of NicńĀŠłćahara is the border; to the west, the reservoir of NandiharipńĀ is the border; in the north, the canal of MollńĀŠĻáa is the border. A portion of land belonging to VelahiŠĻ£ŠĻ≠ńęgrńĀma, demarcated by the four borders as such, measured by a nala which is custom in this region, excluding unprofitable land like that of deity and cattle track, consisting of 120 ńĀŠłćhńĀvńĀpas added with 5 unmńĀnas, production of 150 purńĀŠĻáas in cowries annually, was given by us with forest and bush, with watering place, with ditch and saline land, with areca nut and coconut trees, with ten offences to be tolerated, exempted from all burdens, without entrance of caŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠as and bhaŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠as, without anything taken away, as far as grass field and pasture, to illustrious ń™ŇõvaradevaŇõarman, the ńĀcńĀrya of the great gift of golden horses and chariot, the great grandson of HutńĀŇõanadevaŇõarman, the grandson of MńĀrkandeyadevaŇõarman, the son of LakŠĻ£mńędharadevaŇõarman, belonging to BharadvńĀja gotra and BhńĀradvńĀja, ńÄŠĻÖgirasa and BńĀrhaspatya pravara, performing the observance of Kauthuma ŇõńĀkhńĀ of the SńĀmaveda, on an auspicious day, after pouring water according to rule, in the name of illustrious Lord NńĀrńĀyaŠĻáa, for the increase of merit and fame of parents and myself, after discharging it for being a reward of the given great gift of golden horses and chariot, for the same period as the moon, the sun and the earth exist, by the rule of land reclamation, after making a copperplate edict. Therefore, it should be consented to by you all. And it should also be protected by future kings from fear of hell in case of violation and from respect for merit in case of protection.‚ÄĚ
And here are verses instructing dharma as follows:
The earth was given by many kings beginning with Sagara. To the one to whom the land belongs, belongs then the merit.
The one who gains land and the one who gives land, both of them with meritorious deeds are surely going to heaven.
Either given by himself or given by the others, the one who stole land, he would be boiled with his ancestors after becoming an insect in excreta.
After thus considering wealth and human life fragile as a drop of water on lotus petal, also after knowing all that is said, the meritorious deeds of others should not be destroyed by human beings.
Illustrious LakŠĻ£maŠĻáasena, the king, made NńĀrńĀyaŠĻáadatta sńĀndhivigrahika a messenger here in the donation of the edict to ń™Ňõvara.
Year 2 month BhńĀdra day 28
Conformed by the illustrious king. Confirmed by mahńĀsńĀŠĻĀdhivigrahika.

First notified with an eye copy, transcription and translation by E. Vesey Westmacott. Then re-edited and published by R. D. Banerji first in Bengali and then by him in English. Then re-edited again by N. G. Majumdar99-105. Now re-edited by Ryosuke Furui, based on the digital photographs provided by Bangiya Sahitya Parishat.
